title: 10 best herbal capsules for oily hair

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sesame capsules helps with oily hair because they contain sesamin, a potent natural compound that has been shown to reduce oil production in the scalp. This helps to combat excessive sebum secretion, leaving hair looking healthier and more manageable. The capsules also have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to soothe an itchy or flaky scalp often associated with oily hair. As a result, herbal sesame capsules provide a natural solution for achieving a balanced and healthy-looking mane.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant property: Sesame capsules contain antioxidants that help combat free radicals, which can contribute to oily scalp and hair problems by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress.

Anti-inflammatory property: The anti-inflammatory compounds in sesame capsules can help reduce redness and irritation on the scalp, which can be triggered by oily hair, and promote healthy blood flow to the hair follicles.

Sebum-regulating property: Sesame oil has been traditionally used to regulate sebum production, helping to balance the natural oils of the scalp and hair, thereby reducing excessive oiliness and promoting a healthier, more manageable hair texture.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, sesame roots help reduce dandruff and itching associated with oily hair.
Seeds: High in omega-6 fatty acids, sesame seeds nourish and moisturize the scalp, controlling oil production and promoting healthy hair growth.
remedy_recipe: Grind 2 cups of dried sesamum indicum seeds into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix the ground sesame seed powder with 1 cup of distilled water in a saucepan over low heat for 10 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean container, discarding the solids.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with 500 milligrams of the resulting liquid extract and seal tightly.
Store the herbal sesame capsules in an airtight container in a cool, dry place for up to 6 months before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Aloe capsules helps with oily hair because they contain natural ingredients that balance the scalp's oil production. The aloe vera gel in the capsules helps to regulate sebum levels, reducing excess oil and product buildup. This leaves hair feeling cleaner and lighter, without stripping it of its natural moisture. As a result, users may experience improved manageability, reduced greasiness, and a more balanced scalp environment, making it an effective supplement for those with oily hair.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Aloe vera's anti-inflammatory property helps reduce redness and irritation associated with oily scalp, promoting a healthy environment for hair growth.

Antimicrobial property: Herbal aloe capsules' antimicrobial property helps eliminate bacteria, fungi, and yeast that contribute to oily buildup on the scalp, preventing clogged pores and reducing dandruff.

Moisturizing property: Aloe vera's moisturizing property helps balance the scalp's natural moisture levels, reducing oil production and preventing dryness, itchiness, and flakiness associated with oily hair.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in vitamins, minerals, and antioxidants, aloe vera leaves help regulate sebum production and reduce inflammation in the scalp.
Roots: Aloe vera roots have anti-inflammatory properties that soothe and calm irritated scalps, reducing dandruff and flaking.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 200g of aloe vera gel from mature leaves and puree it in a blender until smooth.
Combine 100g of gel with 20g of glycerin and mix well to enhance moisture retention and absorption.
Add 10g of vitamin e oil and stir thoroughly to provide antioxidant properties and nourishment.
Fill empty capsules with the herbal mixture, ensuring each capsule contains an equal amount of 500mg and seal securely.
Store the herbal aloe capsules in an airtight container for up to 6 months and take one capsule twice daily with water.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Rosemary capsules helps with oily hair because they stimulate blood flow to the scalp, promoting healthy circulation and reducing oil production. The antioxidants and essential oils present in rosemary also help to cleanse and purify the hair follicles, removing impurities and toxins that can contribute to excess oiliness. As a result, rosemary capsules can help to balance the scalp's natural oil production, leaving your hair feeling cleaner, lighter, and more manageable.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant Properties: Rosemary's antioxidant properties help to combat free radicals that can damage hair follicles, leading to excessive oil production and an oily scalp.
Stimulant Properties: Rosemary's stimulant properties increase blood flow to the scalp, which helps to remove impurities and toxins that can contribute to oily hair and dandruff.

Antifungal and Antibacterial Properties: Rosemary's antifungal and antibacterial properties help to combat fungal and bacterial infections on the scalp, which can cause flaking, itchiness, and oil production.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, rosemary roots help regulate sebum production and reduce dandruff.
Leaves: Known for their astringent properties, rosemary leaves can help tighten pores and reduce oiliness, making them an effective ingredient in oily hair treatments.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried rosemary leaves from a reliable source or purchase them from a reputable vendor weighing approximately 2 ounces per serving.
Grind 1 tablespoon of dried rosemary leaves into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle for 30 seconds to 1 minute.
Mix the ground rosemary powder with 500 milligrams of gelatin capsules and fill them by hand or use an automated capsule filler for about 10-15 minutes.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container at room temperature away from direct sunlight for up to 6 months.
Take two capsules containing 500 milligrams of rosemary powder twice daily with food to help control oily hair within 3-6 weeks.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Stinging nettle capsules helps with oily hair because they reduce inflammation and regulate oil production in the scalp. The capsules contain compounds that help to normalize sebum levels, leaving your hair looking healthy and balanced. By reducing excess oil production, stinging nettle capsules can help to prevent greasiness and itchiness, making them an effective natural remedy for oily hair. Additionally, their anti-inflammatory properties can soothe irritated scalps and promote a healthy environment for hair growth.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Nettle capsules contain antioxidants that help neutralize free radicals, which can damage the hair follicles and lead to oil production, resulting in oily hair.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds in nettle capsules can reduce inflammation in the scalp, which is often associated with conditions like dandruff and seborrheic dermatitis that can contribute to oily hair.
Antihormonal properties: Nettle capsules have been shown to regulate hormone imbalances, which can affect oil production in the scalp and lead to oily hair.
remedy_parts: Roots: Stinging nettle roots are rich in antioxidants and have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in reducing inflammation and soothing irritated scalps associated with oily hair.

Seeds: Nettle seeds are a natural source of omega-3 fatty acids and vitamins A and E, which help to nourish the scalp and balance the production of sebum, reducing oiliness and promoting healthy hair growth.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh stinging nettle leaves and flowers in early morning when dew is still present for optimal potency.
Dry the harvested nettle material using a food dehydrator on low setting at 55°c for 6-8 hours.
Combine 200g of dried stinging nettle with 400ml of organic vodka or grain alcohol in a clean glass jar and steep for 4 weeks.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into another container, discarding solids and retaining liquid extract.
Fill empty vegetarian capsules with 500mg of the resulting nettle extract and seal them immediately to preserve potency for up to 2 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tea tree capsules helps with oily hair because they contain potent antioxidants that penetrate the scalp, reducing excess oil production. The antiseptic properties of tea tree oil also help to control fungal and bacterial growth, which can contribute to an oily scalp. Additionally, the capsules' natural astringent properties help to balance the scalp's pH levels, reducing the appearance of greasiness and leaving hair feeling clean and refreshed. Overall, tea tree capsules provide a holistic approach to managing oily hair, promoting a healthier and more balanced scalp.
remedy_properties: Tea Tree Oil: Has antibacterial and antifungal properties that help to reduce scalp irritation, inflammation, and fungal infections that can contribute to oily hair.
Melaleuca Alternifolia: Contains compounds that have antiseptic and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help to soothe and calm the scalp, reducing oil production and dandruff.
Cineole: Has antimicrobial properties that help to kill bacteria and fungi that can cause excessive oil secretion in the scalp, promoting a healthier hair follicle environment.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Known for their antifungal and antibacterial properties, tea tree leaves help to control oil production and reduce inflammation in oily scalp.
Seeds: Rich in antioxidants and fatty acids, tea tree seeds can help to balance the scalp's natural moisture levels and reduce dandruff.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried melaleuca alternifolia leaves from a reliable source ensuring purity and quality.
Grind the dried leaves into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle or grinder.
Mix 500 milligrams of powdered tea tree leaves with 200 milliliters of distilled water to create an infusion solution.
Steep the mixture for 30 minutes at room temperature allowing the active compounds to dissolve.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the cooled infusion solution and store in a cool dark place for up to six months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Wormwood capsules helps with oily hair because they contain Artemisia absinthium, a natural antifungal and antibacterial agent that targets the root cause of excessive oil production. The capsules work to balance the scalp's natural pH levels, reducing inflammation and slowing down sebum production. This results in reduced oiliness and a healthier scalp, leaving hair looking cleaner, shinier, and more manageable. With regular use, wormwood capsules can help restore balance to oily hair and promote a healthy, balanced scalp.
remedy_properties: Astringent properties: Wormwood's astringent compounds help to tighten the pores on the scalp, reducing oil production and absorption, which can help alleviate excessive oiliness.

Antimicrobial properties: Wormwood's antimicrobial activity helps to combat scalp infections and fungal imbalances that can contribute to oily hair by inhibiting the growth of bacteria and fungi that feed on excess sebum.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Wormwood's anti-inflammatory compounds reduce redness, itching, and irritation associated with oily hair, while also soothing the scalp and promoting a healthy environment for hair growth.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in tannins, which help to tighten and close pores on the scalp, reducing oil production.
Barks: Contain a compound called absinthin, which has antifungal and antibacterial properties that can help control dandruff and other scalp issues associated with oily hair.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality artemisia absinthium leaves and flowers, ensuring they are free of contaminants and pesticides.
Dry the harvested plant material in a warm oven or dehydrator for 1-2 hours to reduce moisture content.
Grind the dried plant material into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle, yielding approximately 20 grams.
Mix the powdered wormwood with 400 milliliters of coconut oil and store in an airtight container for at least 2 weeks to allow infusing.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into empty capsules, filling each capsule with approximately 250 milligrams of the infused oil preparation.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sage capsules helps with oily hair because they contain antioxidants that help to balance the scalp's natural oil production. The herbal extract also has astringent properties, which can help to dry out excess sebum and reduce shine. Additionally, sage has anti-inflammatory properties that can soothe an itchy scalp and calm irritation caused by oily hair. By taking sage capsules regularly, individuals with oily hair may find their locks looking healthier, more manageable, and less prone to oiliness.
remedy_properties: Antibacterial properties: Sage's antibacterial compounds, such as camphor and terpenes, help eliminate bacteria on the scalp that can contribute to oil production and clogged pores.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Sage's anti-inflammatory compounds, including flavonoids and phenolic acids, reduce redness and irritation in the scalp, which can help alleviate flaking and itchiness associated with oily hair.

Antioxidant properties: Sage's antioxidants, such as rosmarinic acid, neutralize free radicals that can damage hair follicles and contribute to excessive oil production, promoting healthy hair growth and reducing dandruff.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of sage contain sesquiterpenes and other compounds that help to reduce inflammation and regulate oil production in the scalp, making them effective in controlling oily hair.
Seeds: Sage seeds have been traditionally used to treat scalp conditions such as dandruff and itchiness, which are common issues associated with oily hair.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried salvia officinalis leaves from a trusted source ensuring organic quality and sufficient quantity for the desired batch size.
Grind 20 grams of dried sage leaves into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle to facilitate blending with carrier ingredients.
Mix the powdered sage with 80 grams of finely ground vegetable capsule filler material in a large bowl to achieve an optimal blend ratio.
Fill empty vegetarian capsules with approximately 0.5 grams of the sage-capsule filler mixture using a powder funnel or scoop and repeat process until all materials are consumed.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to six months or refrigerate for extended shelf life ensuring consistent potency and quality.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold capsules helps with oily hair because they contain calendula, a natural herb that has anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. These properties help to soothe and calm an irritated scalp, reducing inflammation that can contribute to excess oil production. Additionally, calendula's antioxidant properties help to balance the scalp's pH levels, regulating sebum production and leaving hair looking healthy and manageable. This natural approach helps to reduce frizz, flyaways, and dandruff, leaving you with a balanced and beautiful mane.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Pot marigold capsules contain flavonoids and carotenoids that reduce inflammation in the scalp, which can contribute to oiliness by causing irritation and redness.
Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants in pot marigold capsules help protect the scalp from damage caused by free radicals, which can lead to an imbalance in sebum production and oily hair.
Astringent properties: Pot marigold capsules contain tannins that help reduce excess oil secretion by tightening the pores of the scalp and balancing the natural moisture levels.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the pot marigold plant have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in reducing inflammation and irritation associated with oily hair.
Barks: The barks of the pot marigold plant contain antifungal agents that help to combat dandruff and itchiness caused by oily hair.
remedy_recipe: Harvest calendula officinalis flowers at peak bloom in the early morning for maximum potency and freshness.
Dry the fresh flowers in a warm and dry place or using a food dehydrator on low heat for 6 hours.
Grind dried calendula officinalis flowers into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle for optimal extraction.
Mix 2000mg of powdered calendula officinalis with 100mg of gelatin in a small bowl and add 20ml of warm water to create a smooth mixture.
Fill empty capsules with the herbal mixture and seal immediately, storing them in an airtight container for up to 2 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree capsules helps with oily hair because they contain a unique blend of antioxidants and essential fatty acids that help to balance the scalp's natural oil production. The capsules' potent ingredients work to reduce sebum secretion, leaving the hair feeling lighter and less greasy. Additionally, the anti-inflammatory properties in maidenhair tree help to soothe an irritated scalp, reducing flakiness and itchiness associated with oily hair.
remedy_properties: Adiantum majus Extract: Contains antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that help to balance oil production in the scalp, reducing excess sebum and promoting healthy hair growth.
Vitamin B Complex: Provides essential nutrients for healthy hair follicles, helping to regulate sebum production and strengthen hair roots, leading to less oily and more manageable locks.
Astringent Tannins: Help to absorb excess moisture and reduce oiliness by tightening the pores on the scalp, making it ideal for controlling oily hair.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, roots help to soothe and calm the scalp, reducing oil production and promoting a healthy hair environment.
Barks: Containing natural astringents, barks help to balance the scalp's natural pH, controlling excess oil secretion and clarifying the hair.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50 grams of ginkgo biloba leaves from mature trees.
Dry the harvested leaves in a single layer for at least 72 hours.
Grind 30 grams of dried ginkgo biloba leaves into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix the powdered ginkgo biloba with 200 milliliters of distilled water to create a liquid extract.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with 500 milligrams of the ginkgo biloba liquid extract and store in an airtight container.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Purple coneflower capsules helps with oily hair because they contain potent antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties that help to balance the scalp's natural pH, reducing excessive oil production. The herbs in these capsules also possess astringent qualities, which absorb excess moisture and purify the pores, leaving your locks feeling cleaner and lighter. Additionally, the antioxidants present in purple coneflower capsules help to strengthen hair follicles, promoting healthy hair growth and a reduced appearance of greasiness.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Purple coneflower's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as artemesinin and luteolin, can help reduce inflammation in the scalp, which may contribute to oily hair by soothing irritation and promoting a healthy balance of sebum production.

Antifungal and antibacterial properties: The capsules' antifungal and antibacterial agents, including triterpenoids and flavonoids, can help combat fungal and bacterial overgrowth on the scalp, which can lead to an imbalance in the natural oil-producing processes, resulting in oily hair.

Antioxidant properties: Purple coneflower's high antioxidant content, comprising vitamins C and E, beta-carotene, and other polyphenols, helps neutralize free radicals that can damage hair follicles, leading to scalp irritation, itchiness, and excess oil production.
remedy_parts: Roots: Contain compounds that help regulate sebum production, reducing oiliness and promoting a healthy scalp.
Seeds: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory agents, which help soothe and calm irritated scalps while controlling excess oil.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh echinacea purpurea flowers and leaves and dry them in a warm place for at least two weeks.
Combine one part dried echinacea purpurea flowers and leaves with one part dried green tea leaves and grind into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix 1 tablespoon of the herbal powder blend with 250ml of cold water to create a tea infusion that is allowed to steep for 10 minutes.
Strain the tea infusion through a cheesecloth or coffee filter to separate the liquid from the solids, then transfer the liquid to a saucepan and bring it to a boil.
Reduce heat and simmer the herbal tea liquid for 30 minutes before filling empty gelatin capsules with 500mg of the cooled liquid and sealing them tightly.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile capsules helps with oily hair because they promote a healthy scalp by reducing inflammation and soothing irritation. The calming properties of chamomile also help to regulate sebum production, balancing the natural oils in the hair and preventing overproduction. As a result, hair appears healthier and less greasy, while also being more manageable and easier to style. Additionally, chamomile's antifungal and antibacterial properties help to control dandruff and other scalp issues that can contribute to oily hair.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Chamomile capsules can reduce inflammation on the scalp, which can help to calm and soothe irritated skin caused by oily hair, leading to a reduction in flaking and redness.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in chamomile capsules help to protect the hair follicles from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to oily hair and dandruff, promoting healthier and balanced hair growth.

Astringent properties: Chamomile's astringent properties help to tighten and constrict the pores on the scalp, reducing oil production and absorption, resulting in a more balanced and manageable amount of oil in the hair.
remedy_parts: Roots: Chamomile roots are rich in antioxidants and have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in reducing oil production and soothing scalp irritations.
Flowers: Chamomile flowers are a natural antiseptic and antibacterial agent, helping to control fungal and bacterial growth on the scalp and regulate sebum production.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried matricaria chamomilla flowers from a reputable supplier or grow your own.
Measure out 50 grams of dried flowers and grind them into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix the powdered flowers with 100 grams of pure gelatin in a bowl to form a uniform blend.
Fill empty #00 capsules with 200 milligrams of the blended mixture and seal each capsule securely.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container for up to six months or use immediately.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: St John's Wort capsules help with oily hair because they have natural astringent and anti-inflammatory properties. The herb has been used for centuries to balance the skin and scalp, reducing excess oil production and controlling sebum secretions. By taking St John's Wort capsules regularly, individuals can experience a reduction in greasiness and dandruff, leaving their hair looking healthier and more manageable. Additionally, the antioxidant properties of the herb help to soothe and calm the scalp, promoting a healthy environment for hair growth.
remedy_properties: Hyperforin: Hyperforin is a potent antioxidant that helps to reduce sebum production by inhibiting the activity of the enzyme 5-alpha-reductase, which converts testosterone into dihydrotestosterone (DHT). This reduction in sebum production can help to alleviate oily hair.

Flavonoids: St John's Wort contains flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help to soothe and calm irritated scalps and reduce redness and flakiness associated with oily hair conditions such as dandruff or psoriasis.

Hypolipidemic activity: St John's Wort has been shown to possess hypolipidemic activity, meaning it helps to lower cholesterol levels in the blood. This can help to reduce the amount of sebum produced by the scalp, leading to healthier, less oily hair.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, roots help to regulate oil production and reduce inflammation in the scalp.
Leaves: Containing salicylic acid and other beta-hydroxy acids, leaves help to exfoliate and unclog pores, reducing excess oil and acne.
Flowers: High in anthraquinones, flowers possess anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which soothe and calm irritated scalps prone to oily hair.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of fresh st john's wort flowers at peak blooming stage for optimal potency.
Dry the harvested flowers in a warm dark place or using a food dehydrator on low heat.
Grind the dried flowers into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix 20g of ground st john's wort with 10g of silica gel capsules and store in an airtight container.
Store the filled capsules in a cool dark place for at least 2 weeks to allow the herbs to mature.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemongrass capsules helps with oily hair because they contain citral, a natural compound that has antifungal and antibacterial properties. When consumed as supplements, lemongrass capsules help to regulate sebum production in the scalp, reducing excessive oil secretion and controlling dandruff and itchiness. This leads to a reduction in greasiness and a healthy, balanced hair growth. Additionally, lemongrass has natural astringent properties that help to tighten pores, further minimizing oil buildup and leaving hair looking clean and refreshed.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial Properties: Lemongrass capsules contain compounds that have antimicrobial properties, which can help control the growth of bacteria and fungi on the scalp, reducing oil production and preventing clogged pores.
Anti-Inflammatory Effects: Lemongrass contains anti-inflammatory compounds that can soothe and calm an itchy or irritated scalp, reducing redness and inflammation associated with oily hair.

Astringent Properties: The astringent properties of lemongrass capsules can help tighten the hair follicles and pores, reducing excess oil secretion and controlling sebum production, leading to healthier-looking hair.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes are good for making medicinal capsules because they contain a high concentration of bioactive compounds that help to regulate sebum production and reduce dandruff.
Roots: Roots are used due to their ability to absorb excess oil from the scalp, reducing greasiness and promoting healthy hair growth.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of fresh cymbopogon citratus leaves from your garden or local market and clean them thoroughly with distilled water.
Dry the cleaned leaves in a low-temperature oven at 60°c for 12 hours or until crispy, then grind into a fine powder using a spice grinder.
Mix 20g of the ground cymbopogon citratus powder with 80g of microcrystalline cellulose and 10g of silicon dioxide in a clean bowl.
Fill empty capsules shells with the prepared blend and seal according to manufacturer's instructions, yielding 100 capsules per batch.
Store the finished capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 6 months or refrigerate for up to 1 year, using one capsule twice daily as needed.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Allspice capsules helps with oily hair because they contain berberine, a natural compound that reduces sebum production in the scalp. Berberine works by inhibiting the activity of an enzyme called 5-alpha-reductase, which is responsible for converting testosterone into dihydrotestosterone (DHT), a hormone that contributes to oiliness and dandruff. By reducing DHT levels, berberine helps to regulate sebum production, leaving hair looking healthier, cleaner, and less greasy. Regular use of allspice capsules can lead to a noticeable reduction in oily hair and scalp issues.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Allspice contains antioxidants like flavonoids, which help neutralize free radicals that can contribute to oil production and clogged pores on the scalp.
Astringent properties: The astringent compounds in allspice, such as tannins, help constrict blood vessels and reduce sebum secretion, leading to a decrease in oily hair.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Allspice's anti-inflammatory compounds, like eugenol, can soothe and calm the scalp, reducing inflammation that can contribute to oil production and promoting healthy hair growth.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the allspice plant are rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that help to reduce sebum production and balance the scalp.
Buds: Allspice buds contain flavonoids and terpenes that have antiseptic and astringent properties, helping to purify the scalp and regulate oil secretion.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh pimenta dioica leaves measuring 100g for subsequent drying.
Dry the pimenta dioica leaves in a warm dry place for 3 days taking regular turns.
Grind the dried pimenta dioica leaves into a fine powder using a spice grinder.
Mix the ground pimenta dioica with 200ml of distilled water and let it steep for 2 hours at room temperature.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth and fill empty gelatin capsules with 250mg of the herbal extract for dosing.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fennel capsules help with oily hair because they contain compounds that stimulate the scalp and improve circulation, reducing excess oil production. The antioxidants present in fennel also help to neutralize free radicals that can damage hair follicles, leading to healthier and more balanced locks. Additionally, fennel's astringent properties help to tighten pores on the scalp, reducing sebum buildup and leaving hair feeling fresh and clean. Anti-inflammatory properties: Fennel's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as flavonoids and terpenes, help soothe an itchy scalp and reduce redness, which can occur when hair is excessively oily.

Detoxifying properties: Fennel's natural diuretic and anti-inflammatory properties may help remove impurities and excess sebum from the scalp, reducing oil production and promoting a healthy hair growth environment.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in fiber and antioxidants, which help to absorb excess oil and reduce inflammation in the scalp.
Seeds: High in fatty acids, particularly omega-3 and omega-6, which balance sebum production and promote healthy hair growth.
remedy_recipe: Harvest and dry 50 grams of foeniculum vulgare seeds.
Grind the dried seeds into a fine powder using a spice grinder.
Mix the powdered seeds with 100 grams of gelatin and 50 grams of water.
Allow the mixture to infuse for 30 minutes and then heat it slowly until the gelatin dissolves.
Fill empty capsules with 500 milligrams of the resulting mixture and seal them tightly.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Broad-leaved lime capsules helps with oily hair because they contain antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that help to regulate oil production in the scalp. The capsules also possess antimicrobial properties, which prevent the growth of bacteria and other microorganisms that can contribute to oily hair. Barks: Contains tannins and flavonoids that aid in reducing excess oil secretion and tightening pores.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh leaves of tilia platyphyllos flowers in the morning for maximum potency.
Dry the harvested leaves using a food dehydrator at 60°c for 6 hours or air-drying for 7 days.
Grind the dried leaves into a fine powder using a coffee grinder and store them in an airtight container.
Mix 1000mg of ground tilia platyphyllos with 500mg of silica gel and 200mg of vegetable glycerin, then fill into capsules.
Store the finished herbal capsules in an airtight container in a cool dark place for up to 2 years.

Grind 50g of the dried flowers into a fine powder using a spice grinder.
Mix the ground flower powder with 250ml of distilled water to create an infusion.
Steep the mixture for 30 minutes and then strain it through a cheesecloth or coffee filter.
Fill 10 empty gelatin capsules with 250mg each of the infused liquid.

Astringent Properties: The astringent compounds in Herbal Prairie Toothpick Capsules help to tighten pores, reducing excess oil secretion and leaving hair feeling cleaner and less greasy.
Antioxidant Properties: Antioxidants in the capsules help to neutralize free radicals that can damage hair follicles, leading to increased sebum production and oily hair.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in saponins, which help to balance oil production and reduce sebum secretion.
Rhyzomes: Contains anti-inflammatory compounds that soothe the scalp and reduce redness associated with oily hair.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh zanthoxylum americanum bark from sustainable sources ensuring 50-70 grams of dried material for the batch.
Dry the harvested bark using air circulation and temperature control methods taking approximately 7 days to reach optimal dryness.
Grind the dried zanthoxylum americanum bark into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle or spice grinder achieving a uniform consistency.
Mix the powdered zanthoxylum americanum with 150-200 grams of natural silica gel and 50-70 grams of tapioca starch for proper filling and encapsulation.
Fill empty vegetarian capsules with the prepared mixture and store them in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 6 months ensuring potency and freshness.

intro_desc: Herbal capsules for oily hair are natural supplements that contain extracts from various plants, such as saw palmetto, biotin, and tea tree oil. These capsules help to regulate sebum production, reducing the greasiness of the scalp and hair. For example, saw palmetto capsules block the conversion of testosterone to dihydrotestosterone (DHT), a hormone that contributes to oily hair. With regular use, these capsules can improve the overall appearance of oily hair by making it look healthier, shinier, and more manageable. This can significantly enhance one's confidence and daily routine.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat oily hair with herbal capsules is to consume them regularly for a period of time. Take a combination of capsules containing herbs like Triphala, Neem, and Amla, which are known for their purifying and balancing properties on the scalp. These herbs help to regulate sebum production, reducing oiliness and promoting healthy hair growth.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to oily hair that are treated with herbal capsules are conditions characterized by excessive sebum production or imbalance in the body's natural oil regulation. These include acne, hyperhidrosis (excessive sweating), and rosacea. Herbal capsules containing ingredients like saw palmetto, guggul, and tea tree oil may help regulate oil production, reduce inflammation, and promote healthy skin and scalp function.
